steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content:
Left side: the hit — noun phrase with determiner + adjective
Right side: 'Oh What a Day' by the Dells — the title and attribution
The blank needs a noun to connect them.

steps.title: Apply the rule for nouns and quotation-mark titles
steps.content: The rule is: a noun and its immediately following quotation-mark title are not separated by punctuation—they form a single unit. D applies this rule correctly by using no punctuation. A, B, and C each violate this rule by adding a colon, comma, or semicolon respectively, incorrectly fragmenting the noun phrase. That matches choice D — D is correct.
